---
title: ""
subtitle: ""
---

![](e4e-logo.svg){fig-align="center" width="450px"}

## Course Staff

**Instructors:** [John List](https://voices.uchicago.edu/jlist/) & [Evan Rose](https://ekrose.github.io/)

**Office Hours:** Upon request (email TAs).

## Logistics

**Lectures:** Tuesday & Thursday, 11:00am–12:20pm

**Location:** Kersten Physics Teaching Center 106

**Section**: Thursday, 5:30-6:20pm and 6:30-7:20pm, location TBA

## Course Description

Because modern economics puts such a heavy stress on technical rigor, the most interesting economic ideas are often buried behind abstract mathematical notation. Professors List and Rose will explore these big ideas without the math, making the beauty and power of economic thinking available to everyone.

We will discuss what it means to think like an economist, how you can use economic thinking to make the world a better place, and how economists think about today's biggest issues, including what makes your rent get more expensive every year, why discrimination persists (and how to fix it), and how generative artificial intelligence will reshape the labor market.

## Prerequisites

No prior economics background required.

## Assignments

Each lecture will be accompanied by readings and/or podcasts and videos. You are required to complete these prior to the lecture (including Week 1). Details on weekly assignments are available on the [Assignments](assignments.qmd) tab.

You will also complete quizzes and polls in class using Poll Everywhere. You can sign up as a participant [here](https://id.polleverywhere.com/signup?usrc=login_page). 

Your final grade will depend on the following. 

- In-class participation quizzes (20%)
- Weekly AI-enabled discussions (20%)
- Two midterms (30%)
- Final project (30%)
